name: SCRED Speed & Throughput Optimization - Full Stack

description: Baseline: Streaming redaction throughput (10MB with 64KB chunks)

asi.hypothesis: Establish baseline for full-stack optimization: detector, redactor, CLI, MITM, proxy

description: Optimization 1: Avoid cloning lookahead buffer using std::mem::take

asi.hypothesis: Avoid expensive Vec clone of lookahead buffer - reuse capacity with std::mem::take

description: Optimization 2: Use in-place byte-level redaction (34.8% faster)

asi.hypothesis: Use in_place byte-level redaction instead of string-based redaction for speed
asi.improvement_notes: Avoids String allocation and UTF8 conversion in hot path, uses byte-level detect_all + redact_in_place

description: Analysis: Realistic workload measurement (1 secret per 100KB) shows 101.5 MB/s

asi.finding: Performance is BETTER on realistic data (101.5 MB/s) than dense test data (66.3 MB/s)
asi.hypothesis: Realistic workload has sparse patterns - detector overhead is minimal
asi.implication: Current optimizations work well on real workloads, sparse data is actually faster
asi.recommendation: Validate all future optimizations on both dense and sparse workload mixes

description: Optimization 3: Early rejection & max_len limit in validation detector (+129%!)

asi.hypothesis: Add early rejection in validation detector: check min_len before scan_token_end, limit scan to max_len
asi.improvement_notes: Avoids expensive scan_token_end calls for invalid tokens and limits scanning depth

description: Verification: Realistic mixed workload now 169.8 MB/s (+67%)

asi.hypothesis: Early rejection optimizes sparse pattern detection as well as dense
asi.implication: Both benchmark and real workloads benefit significantly

description: Optimization 4: Limit simple_prefix token scan depth (+4%)

asi.hypothesis: Limit simple_prefix scan depth to MAX_SIMPLE_TOKEN_LEN (256) bytes
asi.improvement_notes: API keys rarely exceed 256 bytes; limits unnecessary scanning
